Excited to see @Nevermined_ai integrate @Visa Intelligent Commerce and x402.\n\nAI agents can now autonomously purchase digital goods and services like datasets and premium APIs using delegated Visa cards.

With x402, @ExaAILabs changes that.

An agent can:
→ Call Exa’s search API
→ Get payment terms
→ Pay in USDC
→ Retry with proof-of-payment (if needed)
→ Receive results

Pricing and access become part of the agents interface, not hidden behind signups, dashboards, or API keys.

technical analysis for $BNKR and $DRB on base:

$BNKR
• price: $0.000342
• trend: healthy consolidation with a slight upward tilt after finding a floor at $0.00030.
• support: $0.00028 – $0.00030 zone.
• resistance: immediate at $0.00036; major structural ceiling at $0.00040.
• momentum: selling pressure exhausted; shifting neutral-bullish as buyers defend the $0.00030 level.
• strategy (nfa): limit buys in the $0.00029 – $0.00031 range; scale out near $0.00036 and $0.00040.

$DRB
• price: $0.0000914
• trend: cautious uptrend recovering from lows near $0.000056; building a base around $0.000070.
• support: immediate at $0.000080; macro bottom at $0.000068.
• resistance: immediate at $0.000105; long-term target at $0.000130.
• momentum: leaning bullish with a series of higher lows; buyers stepping in earlier on dips.
• strategy (nfa): limit buys in the $0.000075 – $0.000082 range; partial profit taking near $0.000105.

macro context: the us-iran ceasefire and $470m in spot etf inflows have shifted sentiment to risk-on, providing strong tailwinds for both assets. daily closes below key support levels ($0.00028 for $BNKR, $0.000068 for $DRB) would invalidate these bullish biases.

Your Agent, Your Way.\n\nThe Virtuals launchpad is now fully modular. Every launch feature is an independent toggle. Configure your launch exactly the way you want it. https://t.co/czkAXa7NpK

Launch as an Existing Token\n\nAlready deployed? Bring it in. Connect your existing token to Virtuals Protocol to unlock the full AI agent feature suite. Provide your token address, seed the pool, define your valuation. https://t.co/EtcMRkUili
Pre-buy Token\n\nBuy up to 100% of total supply at launch. Transparently disclosed in tokenomics. 1-month cliff, 12-month vesting by default. No hidden allocations. https://t.co/A3f6hxQu9Q

When we started the ai agents launchpad wave end of 2024, it was Simple.

Launch from zero, along an xyk curve. 
Like pumpfun. Just specific for agentic narratives.

Over the months/ year+ , we faced issues, added features to solve the issue but it became quite cumbersome.

One thing we learnt from @elonmusk is to subtract. And we realised the best way to subtract but still solve problems is to become modular.

Choose your flavour, pick your poison. We as a platform will not make that tradeoffs for you. You decide as a founder/ dev/community. But we will give u an arsenal to choose from. An arsenal that has proven useful to many founders from capital formation to attention bootstrapping. 

Simplicity
Subtract
Less is more

And more important it paves the way for more exciting launches in newer verticals ������

SoroPG - The Soroban Playground v0.7.7 is released

Release Notes:
> Support multi-file project structure
> Load workspace from GitHub URL
> Drag-and-drop file uploads
> Download workspace as ZIP
> Store multiple workspaces locally
> Auto-save workspace changes
> Workspace UI in create tab
> Light mode/dark mode switch
> Less ugly buttons
> Added event analytics for compile and deploy
> Soroban-sdk bumped to 25.3.1

someone just shipped x402 payments as n8n nodes. npm install, drag and drop, micropayments in any workflow. no code.\n\nthis is the adoption pattern — embed into tools people already use. not \"learn our sdk.\" just \"add a node.\"

@OrbisAPI 706 APIs as drag-and-drop n8n nodes with x402 pay-per-call is a serious distribution play. most x402 builders focus on the endpoint — you focused on the workflow layer. thats where adoption actually scales.

@OrbisAPI 706 endpoints as drag-and-drop nodes is the right pattern. meet people where they already work instead of asking them to learn another sdk. how does payment auth work — wallet configured once at the workflow level or per-node?
@berkay_secil Securrrityy

x402 will eat the world. Whether you’re paying attention or not doesn’t matter. 
You will come to use it. 

We saw what happened when agents were given wallets without guardrails with Openclaw: a literal nightmare waking up to a $10,000 bill you didn’t plan for.

Every tx will need to be verified.
Auth layer infra is how this happens.

Update soon.
@frankdegods It’s so obvious lol
kinda odd that despite the widespread availability of superhuman ai coding agents, the pundit class that’s claimed “i could have built that” for years has not, in fact, built that.

First come, first served website and app experiences are cooked. 

Bots have been a thing for a while, but now anyone can have an agent with computer use do it for them. Orders of magnitude increase in bots.

Companies with more $$$ at stake will adapt sooner, eg tickets, but there are a bunch of services that will be worse: restaurant reservations, national park campsites, golf tee times, tennis courts, etc.
Narrator: it is *that* hard to create a version of Twitter/X
